---
title: "Reproducible analytical workflow template"
output:
  github_document:
    toc: false
    html_preview: false
---

<p align="left">
<strong><a href="README.md">Home</a></strong> |
<strong><a href="R/README.md">R code</a></strong> |
<strong><a href="scripts/README.md">Scripts</a></strong> |
<strong><a href="config/README.md">Config</a></strong> |
<strong><a href="metadata/README.md">Metadata</a></strong> |
<strong><a href="data/README.md">Data</a></strong> |
<strong><a href="outputs/README.md">Outputs</a></strong> |
<strong><a href="docs/README.md">Docs</a></strong>
</p>

<p>
<img style="float: left;" alt="IMARPE logo" src="assets/img/imarpe.png" width="28%" height="28%" hspace="0" />

<img style="float: right;" alt="DIPEL-IMARPE logo" src="assets/img/dipel.png" width="18%" height="18%" hspace="120" />
</p>

<br><br><br><br>

# Reproducible analytical workflow template

This repository provides a standard template for reproducible analytical workflows developed under the **Dirección de Investigaciones del Subsistema Pelágico – Instituto del Mar del Perú (DIPEL-IMARPE)**.

The template is designed for technical analyses involving pelagic resources, fisheries indicators, survey data, stock assessment inputs, spatial-temporal analyses, statistical models, and reproducible scientific outputs.

A repository created from this template should allow users to:

- identify the analytical objective;
- understand the input data sources;
- run the analysis in a controlled sequence;
- reproduce tables, figures, diagnostics, and model outputs;
- document the computational environment;
- audit the relationship between data, code, outputs, and reports.

---

## Repository purpose

This template is intended for repositories classified as:

**Analytical process**

That is, repositories that reproduce a complete technical analysis from documented inputs to validated outputs.

Examples include:

- CPUE standardization;
- selectivity analysis;
- acoustic or biological survey indicators;
- length-composition analysis;
- juvenile indicators;
- environmental indicators;
- spatial-temporal fisheries analysis;
- model diagnostics;
- stock-assessment input preparation.

---

## Directory structure

```text
├── README.md / README.Rmd        : Main description of the repository
├── analysis-template.Rproj       : RStudio project file
├── _targets.R                    : Main reproducible pipeline
├── _quarto.yml                   : Quarto project configuration
├── index.qmd                     : Main technical report
├── audit.qmd                     : Reproducibility and audit report
├── renv.lock                     : R package environment lockfile
├── .gitignore                    : Files and directories ignored by Git
│
├── R                             : Functions used by the analytical workflow
│   ├── 00_packages.R             : Required R packages
│   ├── fn_read_data.R            : Data reading functions
│   ├── fn_clean_data.R           : Data cleaning functions
│   ├── fn_fit_model.R            : Model fitting functions
│   ├── fn_plot_results.R         : Plotting functions
│   └── fn_utils.R                : General utility functions
│
├── scripts                       : Sequential scripts for human-readable execution
│   ├── 00_init.R                 : Initialize the project
│   ├── 01_import.R               : Import data
│   ├── 02_clean.R                : Clean and standardize data
│   ├── 03_explore.R              : Exploratory analysis
│   ├── 04_model.R                : Model fitting or analytical estimation
│   ├── 05_diagnostics.R          : Diagnostics and validation
│   ├── 06_export.R               : Export tables, figures, and model outputs
│   └── 99_render.R               : Render reports
│
├── config                        : Configuration files and analysis parameters
│   ├── params.yml                : General parameters
│   ├── paths.yml                 : Input and output paths
│   └── packages.yml              : Package requirements
│
├── metadata                      : Metadata, data dictionaries, and audit information
│   ├── data_sources.yml          : Description of data sources
│   ├── data_dictionary.csv       : Variable names, units, and descriptions
│   ├── schema.yml                : Expected data structure
│   └── checksums.csv             : File checksums when applicable
│
├── data                          : Local data directory
│   ├── README.md                 : Data policy and data-use instructions
│   ├── example                   : Synthetic or public example data
│   ├── raw                       : Raw data placeholder; usually not committed
│   ├── interim                   : Intermediate data products
│   └── processed                 : Processed analytical inputs
│
├── inputs                        : Model input files when applicable
├── outputs                       : Analytical outputs
│   ├── tables                    : Exported tables
│   ├── figures                   : Exported figures
│   ├── models                    : Fitted models or model objects
│   └── logs                      : Log files
│
├── docs                          : Reports, notes, and rendered documentation
├── reports                       : Quarto reports and technical outputs
├── arc                           : Archive of previous official runs or versions
├── src                           : Compiled source code or external model source files
├── tests                         : Basic tests for functions and workflow checks
│
├── .github                       : GitHub configuration
│   ├── CODEOWNERS                : Code ownership rules
│   ├── pull_request_template.md  : Pull request template
│   └── workflows                 : GitHub Actions workflows
│
└── .devcontainer                 : Optional reproducible computing environment
    ├── Dockerfile
    └── devcontainer.json
```

---

## Workflow logic

This template separates three levels of work:

```text
R/         = reusable functions
scripts/   = sequential analytical scripts
_targets.R = formal reproducible pipeline
```

The recommended analytical sequence is:

```text
00_init.R
01_import.R
02_clean.R
03_explore.R
04_model.R
05_diagnostics.R
06_export.R
99_render.R
```

This sequence is intended for manual review, teaching, debugging, and transparent inspection.

The formal pipeline is controlled by:

```text
_targets.R
```

This file defines the dependency graph of the analysis and allows the full workflow to be regenerated in a controlled and auditable way.

---

## How to run the analysis

### 1. Restore the R environment

```r
renv::restore()
```

### 2. Run the reproducible pipeline

```r
targets::tar_make()
```

### 3. Render the main technical report

```r
quarto::quarto_render("index.qmd")
```

### 4. Render the audit report

```r
quarto::quarto_render("audit.qmd")
```

---

## Alternative sequential execution

For step-by-step execution:

```r
source("scripts/00_init.R")
source("scripts/01_import.R")
source("scripts/02_clean.R")
source("scripts/03_explore.R")
source("scripts/04_model.R")
source("scripts/05_diagnostics.R")
source("scripts/06_export.R")
source("scripts/99_render.R")
```

This mode is useful during development, but official outputs should be generated through the reproducible pipeline.

---

## Description

### R

The `R/` directory contains reusable functions used by the analytical workflow.

Functions should be written as modular units and should avoid hard-coded paths. Each function should have a clear purpose, explicit inputs, and explicit outputs.

Further details are available in:

[ R code documentation ](R/README.md)

---

### Scripts

The `scripts/` directory contains the sequential analytical workflow.

Scripts must be numbered according to their execution order:

```text
00_init.R
01_import.R
02_clean.R
03_explore.R
04_model.R
05_diagnostics.R
06_export.R
99_render.R
```

Each script should call functions from `R/` rather than containing all logic directly inside the script.

Further details are available in:

[ Scripts documentation ](scripts/README.md)

---

### Config

The `config/` directory contains configuration files used to control paths, parameters, package requirements, model options, and analysis settings.

Typical files include:

```text
params.yml
paths.yml
packages.yml
```

Further details are available in:

[ Config documentation ](config/README.md)

---

### Metadata

The `metadata/` directory contains information required to interpret, audit, and reproduce the analysis.

It may include:

```text
data_sources.yml
data_dictionary.csv
schema.yml
checksums.csv
```

Raw data should not be stored in public GitHub repositories. Metadata should describe where the official data are stored, which version was used, and what transformations were applied.

Further details are available in:

[ Metadata documentation ](metadata/README.md)

---

### Data

The `data/` directory is used only for synthetic, public, or local analytical inputs.

Institutional raw data should remain on authorized IMARPE servers or approved storage systems. This repository should not contain confidential, restricted, or sensitive datasets.

Recommended use:

```text
data/example     = synthetic or public example data
data/raw         = local raw data placeholder; usually ignored by Git
data/interim     = intermediate processed objects
data/processed   = processed analytical inputs
```

Further details are available in:

[ Data documentation ](data/README.md)

---

### Inputs

The `inputs/` directory contains model input files when the analysis requires external model formats such as `.dat`, `.ctl`, `.json`, `.csv`, or other structured input files.

This directory is optional and should be used when the analytical workflow generates or consumes formal model input files.

---

### Outputs

The `outputs/` directory stores analytical products generated by the workflow.

Typical outputs include:

```text
outputs/tables
outputs/figures
outputs/models
outputs/logs
```

Official outputs should be linked to a specific Git commit, data version, model configuration, and release tag.

Further details are available in:

[ Outputs documentation ](outputs/README.md)

---

### Reports

The `reports/` directory contains report-specific files, supporting Quarto documents, or additional technical summaries.

The main report is usually:

```text
index.qmd
```

The audit report is:

```text
audit.qmd
```

---

### Docs

The `docs/` directory stores rendered documentation, technical notes, reports, and supporting materials.

Rendered reports can be stored here when required for GitHub Pages, internal documentation, or review.

Further details are available in:

[ Docs documentation ](docs/README.md)

---

### Source code

The `src/` directory is reserved for compiled code, external model source files, or high-performance model components.

Examples include:

```text
.tpl
.cpp
.hpp
.stan
```

This directory is optional and should be used only when the analysis requires compiled or external model code.

---

### Archive

The `arc/` directory stores previous official runs, historical configurations, or archived results.

This directory should not be used as a substitute for Git tags or releases. It is mainly useful when legacy evaluation structures must be preserved for institutional continuity.

---

## Data policy

This repository should not store restricted or confidential raw data.

Recommended separation:

```text
Institutional server = official raw data
GitHub = code, metadata, documentation, configuration, and reproducible workflow
Outputs = derived tables, figures, reports, and model products
```

When data are required to reproduce an analysis, the repository should include metadata describing:

- data source;
- data owner;
- date of extraction;
- version or checksum;
- access restrictions;
- transformation steps;
- responsible analyst.

---

## Reproducibility checklist

Before releasing an official result, verify that:

- [ ] the analysis objective is clearly stated;
- [ ] data sources are documented in `metadata/data_sources.yml`;
- [ ] variables are described in `metadata/data_dictionary.csv`;
- [ ] paths are controlled through `config/paths.yml`;
- [ ] parameters are controlled through `config/params.yml`;
- [ ] functions are stored in `R/`;
- [ ] scripts are numbered and reproducible;
- [ ] `_targets.R` runs without errors;
- [ ] `renv.lock` is updated;
- [ ] `index.qmd` renders correctly;
- [ ] `audit.qmd` renders correctly;
- [ ] outputs are stored in `outputs/`;
- [ ] the final version has a Git tag or release.

---

## Recommended commands

```r
# Restore package environment
renv::restore()

# Inspect the pipeline
targets::tar_visnetwork()

# Run the pipeline
targets::tar_make()

# Render reports
quarto::quarto_render("index.qmd")
quarto::quarto_render("audit.qmd")
```

---

## Contributors

- [qselmer](https://github.com/qselmer)
- [erichdiaz](https://github.com/erichdiaz)
- [ejosymart](https://github.com/ejosymart)

Add or update contributors according to the specific repository created from this template.

---

## Contact

**Dirección de Investigaciones del Subsistema Pelágico**  
**Instituto del Mar del Perú – IMARPE**

- Website: [www.imarpe.gob.pe](http://www.imarpe.gob.pe)
- Contact email: pending

---

## Citation

If this repository supports an official technical product, report, model, or publication, cite the corresponding release, report, or manuscript.

A `CITATION.cff` file should be added when the repository produces reusable scientific software, a formal analytical workflow, or a publication-associated output.
